pluginMechanism: context routing and sandboxed tool output

args:
Benchmark task: routing-read-write-ledger
Repository: https://github.com/honojs/hono.git

Create a new file at bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md. This is a read/write endurance task, not a clever debugging task. First create the bench-artifacts directory if it does not exist. Then read every file in this exact list before writing the final document: src/hono.ts, src/hono-base.ts, src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/common.case.test.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts. The output file must be long but mechanically simple. Start with a title '# Routing Read/Write Ledger'. For each listed file, add a section whose heading is exactly '## <file path>'. Under each heading write three bullets: '- Purpose: ...', '- Names noticed: ...', and '- Simple note: ...'. Keep the notes plain and safe; do not change source code. After the per-file sections, add '## Cross-File Checklist' with one bullet per router family: pattern-router, linear-router, reg-exp-router, smart-router, trie-router. Include exact file paths in the final answer and report that only bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md was created.

Constraints:
- Do not ask the user to write prompts or inspect files manually.
- Prefer the smallest safe code change when edits are required.
- Include exact file paths, commands run, and verification status in the final answer.

commands.command: test -f bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md && grep -q '# Routing Read/Write Ledger' bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md && grep -q '## src/router/trie-router/node.ts' bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md && grep -q '## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts' bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md

artifacts.content:
# Routing Read/Write Ledger

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and provides default routing configuration. Sets up the router instance with a SmartRouter containing RegExpRouter and TrieRouter as fallback options.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, HonoOptions, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema
- Simple note: 35 lines, exports the Hono class with SmartRouter as the default router strategy.

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base module for Hono containing the core Hono class implementation, handler interfaces, routing methods (get, post, put, delete, options, patch, all, on, use), error handling, not-found handling, and mount functionality for other frameworks.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, Hono, HonoOptions, Router, Context, Env, ErrorHandler, NotFoundHandler, HandlerInterface, MiddlewareHandler, compose, getPath, mergePath, METHODS, COMPOSED_HANDLER
- Simple note: 546 lines, comprehensive implementation of the main Hono framework with handler composition and middleware support.

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index for PatternRouter, re-exports the PatternRouter class from the router implementation file.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter
- Simple note: 7 lines, simple index/export file.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter using compiled regular expressions for route matching. Converts path patterns like `:id` and `:id{pattern}` into RegExp and handles wildcard routes.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, Route, Route<T>, emptyParams
- Simple note: 61 lines, uses RegExp compilation for route matching, handles optional parameters with `?` suffix.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index for LinearRouter, re-exports the LinearRouter class from the router implementation file.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter
- Simple note: 7 lines, simple index/export file.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter as a sequential linear search router. Handles static routes, wildcard routes (`*`), and named parameters (`:label`), with optional parameter support via `checkOptionalParameter`.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, ROUTES_LOOP, emptyParams, splitPathRe, splitByStarRe
- Simple note: 145 lines, linear scan algorithm with support for static paths, named parameters, and wildcard patterns.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index for RegExpRouter family, re-exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter along with helper functions for serialization.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
- Simple note: 8 lines, exports the main RegExpRouter and optimized PreparedRegExpRouter for Wasm/deployment scenarios.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter using a trie-based structure that builds compiled regular expressions. Separates middleware and routes, handles wildcard patterns (`*$`), and prepares route data for the PreparedRegExpRouter optimization.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, Router, ParamIndexMap, Matcher, HandlerData, StaticMap, MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, matcher, emptyParam, PATH_ERROR, wild cardRegExpCache, buildWildcardRegExp
- Simple note: 253 lines, builds matchers lazily, caches compiled regexps, supports middleware and wildcard route matching.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Provides the match function and shared types for RegExpRouter's matching logic. Handles static map lookups, RegExp matching with param stash, and matches the router interface contract.
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, Matcher, MatcherMap, emptyParam, match
- Simple note: 34 lines, contains the core match function that interfaces with the trie-based RegExp matching.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter for optimized runtime performance. Pre-processes paths during initialization to build a relocateMap that allows direct route addition without regex rebuilding. Includes serialization for Wasm/deployment bundles.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, MatcherMap, RelocateMap, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, PATH_ERROR, emptyParam
- Simple note: 166 lines, designed for Wasm bundles and static deployment where routes are known upfront.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Node class for the trie-based structure used by RegExpRouter. Each node represents a path segment and builds a compiled regular expression string. Supports labels (`:name`), wildcards (`*`), and parameter mapping.
- Names noticed: Node, Context, ParamAssocArray, PATH_ERROR, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR
- Simple note: 163 lines, builds trie nodes that compile to a single RegExp, handles parameter index mapping.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Trie class that manages a collection of nodes and builds the final compiled regular expression. Handles pattern groups, replacement maps for handler indices and param indices, and constructs the matching RegExp string.
- Names noticed: Trie, Context, ParamAssocArray, ReplacementMap, ReplacementMap, buildRegExp, captureIndex, indexReplacementMap, paramReplacementMap
- Simple note: 75 lines, converts trie structure to a single RegExp with replacement maps for handler resolution.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index for SmartRouter, re-exports the SmartRouter class from the router implementation file.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter
- Simple note: 7 lines, simple index/export file.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter as a router-of-routers fallback mechanism. Tries routers in order (RegExpRouter first, then TrieRouter), commits to the first successful match, and manages router state to switch to the winning router.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Router, Result, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, activeRouter
- Simple note: 71 lines, tries routers sequentially and commits to the first one that successfully matches.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module index for TrieRouter, re-exports the TrieRouter class from the router implementation file.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter
- Simple note: 7 lines, simple index/export file.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements TrieRouter using a trie-based search algorithm. Handles static paths, named parameters (`:label`), wildcards (`*`), and optional parameters via `checkOptionalParameter`. Returns sorted results with middleware taking precedence.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Router, Node, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, Pattern, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, emptyParams, hasChildren
- Simple note: 235 lines, queue-based breadth-first trie traversal with handler score sorting.

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements the Node class for the trie-based router used by TrieRouter. Each node contains children, methods with handler sets, patterns, and params. Supports named parameters, wildcards, and regular expression patterns.
- Names noticed: Node, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, Params, Pattern, METHOD_NAME_ALL, possibleKeys, score, order, children, patterns, params, pushHandlerSets, search
- Simple note: 235 lines, trie node implementation with queue-based search for multi-match support.

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Provides a reusable `runTest` function for shared test cases across all router implementations. Defines test suites for common routing scenarios like basic usage, reserved words, wildcards, named parameters, and complex patterns.
- Names noticed: RunnerTestSuite, ParamIndexMap, Params, Router, runTest, match, skip, Task, suite hierarchy, Basic Usage, Reserved words, Complex, Multi match, Duplicate param name, Optional route, All, Star
- Simple note: 883 lines, comprehensive test suite runner used by all router test files.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for PatternRouter implementation, using the common test runner with skip list for UnsupportedPath errors. Includes specific tests for duplicate param names and trailing slash handling.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, runTest, UnsupportedPathError, skip, Duplicate param name, Trailing slash
- Simple note: 43 lines, routes common tests while testing PatternRouter-specific behaviors.

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for LinearRouter implementation, using the common test runner with skip list for UnsupportedPath errors and trailing slash issues. Tests multi-match `params` scenarios that throw errors.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, runTest, UnsupportedPathError, skip, Multi match, Skip part, Trailing slash
- Simple note: 70 lines, routes common tests while testing LinearRouter-specific limitations.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for RegExpRouter implementation, using the common test runner with skip list for UnsupportedPath errors and ambiguous routes. Includes tests for return value types and specific unsupported configuration scenarios.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, runTest, UnsupportedPathError, ParamStash, Ambiguous, Return value type, Capture Group, static and dynamic
- Simple note: 142 lines, routes common tests with skip list for RegExpRouter-specific unsupported patterns.

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for SmartRouter implementation, using the common test runner with no skip list since SmartRouter delegates to underlying routers. Tests the router-fallback mechanism with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, runTest, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter
- Simple note: 14 lines, very minimal test file as SmartRouter inherits test coverage from delegated routers.

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for TrieRouter implementation, using the common test runner with no skip list. Tests the full trie-based router implementation for all common routing scenarios.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, runTest
- Simple note: 9 lines, routes all common tests with no exclusions.

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Comprehensive unit tests for the TrieRouter Node class itself, not just the router wrapper. Tests root node behavior, wildcard handling, named parameters, regex patterns, multi-match scenarios, duplicate param names, and sort order.
- Names noticed: Node, Root Node, Basic Usage, Name path, Name path - Multiple route, Param prefix, Wildcard, Regexp, All, Special Wildcard, Default with wildcard, Multi match, Duplicate param name, Sort Order, star, Routing order With named parameters, Node with initial method and handler
- Simple note: 817 lines, extensive test coverage for Node class behavior and search algorithm.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

- **pattern-router**: src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts — Compiled RegExp approach, supports `:label` and `:label{pattern}`, no multi-match support, throws UnsupportedPathError for duplicate params.

- **linear-router**: src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts — Linear sequential scan algorithm, supports static routes and wildcards, no multi-match for `params`, allows trailing slash normalization.

- **reg-exp-router**: src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts — Trie-based RegExp compilation, lazy matcher building, Prepared version for Wasm deployment, supports middleware and complex patterns, no duplicate param names.

- **smart-router**: src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts — Router-of-routers fallback mechanism, tries RegExpRouter first then TrieRouter, commits to first successful match, name updates to active router.

- **trie-router**: src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts — Queue-based breadth-first trie traversal, supports full multi-match scenarios, handler scores for middleware priority, extensive Node class test coverage.

---

## File Creation Summary

- **Created file**: bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md
- **Directory created**: bench-artifacts (if not already existing)
- **Files read**: 24 source/test files listed above
- **Command executed**: `mkdir -p bench-artifacts`
- **Verification**: Only bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md was created in the bench-artifacts directory.
